title: Pre-chapter controller split phase
summary: Before the next major chapter expansion, lane 1 should reduce coordinator risk. This wave plans and executes a targeted split of high-concentration season and station logic out of the largest files while keeping behavior stable.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the first controller split handoff.
execution_order.why_now: The coordinator files are large enough that the next chapter wave will get riskier without targeted extraction.

execution_order.goal: Extract the first season or station controller seam.
execution_order.why_now: One focused split can reduce future change cost without redesigning the product.
Refactor work needs strong protection against subtle state regressions.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the second controller split handoff.
execution_order.why_now: Once the first extraction lands, the next highest-risk seam should be isolated while context is fresh.
A second targeted split can keep season-three-scale growth from turning into surgery.
The game is now important enough that structural protection is worth explicit queue space.

guardrails: Do not redesign product behavior during the split.
Preserve saves, route state, and UI behavior.
Prefer targeted domain extraction over broad rewrites.

move_after_sync_boundary: route board, expedition, atlas, archive, and continuity composition
returned selected upgrade and nursery project normalization
verification: npx vitest run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "opens the world-map field station, claims field credit, and buys trail stride|opens the nursery tab and starts one teaching-bed project from the field station|surfaces the season capstone, then opens the next field season on the routes shell"

npx vitest run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "surfaces one active field request in the journal and turns Hidden Hollow notebook-ready after the seep-stone confirm|buys route marker after the movement pair and lets the support row activate it on the world map|shows the thaw-window route replay note when re-entering tundra during the active process window"
